purpose: Distinguish between energy (current account) and agricultural (inflation expectations) channels of commodity-to-FX transmission for proper country-specific risk assessment.
components: Energy channel: operates through current account balance, 1-3 month transmission lag, dominant for energy-intensive economies (JPY, EUR, NOK)
Agricultural channel: operates through inflation expectations and central bank reaction functions, 3-12 month lag, dominant for food-importing EMs (EGP, PKR, BDT, with 30-50% CPI food weights)
Interaction effects: alignment amplifies FX moves 2-3x; conflict partially cancels
Commodity export threshold: ~15% of GDP determines which channel dominates
current_assessment: Energy leg loosening (OPEC+ surplus, oil heading to 0.5-1.0mb/d surplus by Q4 2026). Agricultural leg tightening (thin crop buffers on convex price-stocks curve). Net vulnerability requires integrating both channels with proper weights — currently impossible due to missing framework.
limitations: Quantitative improvement claim (15-25% more REER variance) is unsubstantiated
15% GDP threshold may be an approximation rather than empirically validated (BIS citation vague)
Denominator neglect: partial R² after dollar control never reported (KD6)
Country-specific segmentation by relative energy vs agricultural import shares not performed
